archetype: ARCHETYPE-3 (Runtime / Stateful subsystem) for the primary unit `@netscript/plugin-sagas-core` — it owns the saga engine, ports, and the telemetry seam; F-13 saga/runtime-invariants applies. The `plugins/sagas` deployable + service composition roots are the wiring/integration surface (consumer of the seam), not a new archetype.
scopeOverlays: SCOPE-service (plugins/sagas/services composition root + oRPC publish handler trace linkage)
lockedDecisions: Seam ownership: the vendor-neutral telemetry seam stays in `@netscript/plugin-sagas-core/src/telemetry` (already built). The real OTel-backed `SagaTelemetryTracer` adapter lives in `plugins/sagas` (composition root), which already depends on `@netscript/telemetry`. plugin-sagas-core gains NO @netscript/telemetry or @opentelemetry/api dependency.
Span owner = SagaEngine. The `saga.handle` span is created/ended inside `SagaEngine.#handleEntry`, wrapping the handler + #persistTransition sequence (one span per handled message per instance).

Default = NOOP. `SagaEngineOptions.instrumentation` defaults to `new SagaInstrumentation()` (NOOP tracer), so unwired/library-default usage is inert and behavior-preserving; observability is enabled only when a composition root injects the OTel adapter.

Seam extension (additive, locked now): `SagaTelemetryTracer.startSpan` options and `SagaHandleSpanInput` gain an optional `parent?: SagaTraceParent ({ traceparent?, tracestate? })`; the OTel adapter turns it into a remote OTel parent Context via `extractFromTraceContext`. This is the only seam-shape change and is required for API→engine trace linkage.

Parent context source = `message.traceparent`/`message.tracestate` (already populated by the API publish handler from `getTraceContext()`), turned into an active OTel parent by the adapter. The API publish handler keeps its existing trace-header propagation; the SSE `saga:message_received` event keeps headers as breadcrumb data (no shape change).

Injection points: service `plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63`, `saga-runner` default runtimeOptions, and `saga-supervisor` `createDefaultRuntime` default — all via the existing `runtimeOptions` pass-through, using `createSagaTelemetry()`.
Metrics scope locked to `recordHandleDuration` on the handle path; compensation/dlq/idempotency/concurrency counters and `saga.cascade.*` spans are explicitly deferred (additive, no rework).
Span name `saga.handle` and attribute vocabulary reused verbatim from existing `SagaSpanNames`/`SagaAttributes`; no new vocabulary.
contracts: SagaTraceParent = Readonly<{ traceparent?: string; tracestate?: string }> — NEW exported type on @netscript/plugin-sagas-core/telemetry
SagaHandleSpanInput: add optional `parent?: SagaTraceParent` (additive); startHandleSpan forwards it
SagaEngineOptions: add optional `instrumentation?: SagaInstrumentation` (defaults to NOOP)
SagaRuntimeNativeOptions: add optional `instrumentation?: SagaInstrumentation`; createNativeBus threads it into createSagaEngine + createSagaBusBridge
plugins/sagas: createOtelSagaTracer(): SagaTelemetryTracer and createSagaTelemetry(): SagaInstrumentation (internal composition-root API, not a published @netscript/* export)
testPlan: Unit (plugin-sagas-core, recording SagaTelemetryTracer double): SagaInstrumentation.startHandleSpan forwards `parent` into tracer.startSpan options.

Unit: SagaEngine emits exactly one `saga.handle` span per handled message with attributes saga.id/saga.instance.id/saga.event.type/saga.attempt/saga.durability_tier/saga.correlation_key, state.before+state.after events, and outcome=success on the happy path.

Failure-path unit: a throwing handler yields a span finished with ERROR status + recordException, the error rethrows, and recordHandleDuration is called with outcome=error; span ends in finally (no leak).
Unit: NOOP default — engine with no instrumentation emits no observable telemetry and all existing tests/runtime/* pass unchanged (regression guard).
Unit (plugin-sagas-core): instrumentation injected via createSagaRuntime({ native: { instrumentation } }) reaches the engine and produces a handle span after runtime.publish of a registered message.
Unit (plugins/sagas): createOtelSagaTracer maps kind→SpanKind, parent→remote OTel context, setStatus ok/error→SpanStatusCode, recordException, end — verified against a fake/in-memory OTel tracer.

Integration (plugins/sagas/services): publishing through a runtime built with createSagaTelemetry() against an in-memory OTel span exporter yields a saga.handle span that shares the server span's traceId and is its child (gap 3 closed).
Integration failure-path: throwing handler through the service path yields an ERROR-status saga.handle span with the recorded exception.
planGateSelfCheck: PASS expected. Research present and re-baselined to current main with verified file:line for all three gaps (none resolved). Decisions locked with rationale (seam ownership, NOOP default, span owner, additive parent extension, injection points, metrics scope). Open-decision sweep: the parent-context seam extension is resolved-now (deferring forces engine+adapter rework → would FAIL_PLAN if left open), and metrics/cascade scope is marked safe-to-defer (additive, no rework). Commit slices: 5, ordered, <30, each names what it proves, its gate, and touched files. Risk register present with mitigations. Gate set selected from archetype-gate-matrix (Archetype 3 + SCOPE-service): static check/lint/fmt via .llm/tools wrappers, targeted tests, F-13/F-3/F-5/F-6/F-15/F-18 manual evidence; e2e:cli correctly excluded (no scaffold output change). Deferred scope explicit. jsr-audit applied to the planned surface: additive optional fields + one concrete exported type on ./telemetry, no slow types, OTel adapter not a published surface. Residual risk: none material; the only judgment call is the additive `parent` field, which is locked now.
openQuestions: In-memory OTel span exporter availability for the integration test: prefer @netscript/telemetry test utils / @opentelemetry/sdk-trace-base InMemorySpanExporter; if neither is wired in plugins/sagas test deps, fall back to a fake OTel Tracer recording span ops. Resolve at S5 implementation; does not affect contracts.
risks: Active-context propagation across the synchronous handler boundary: SagaHandler is synchronous, but #persistTransition is async. Mitigation: the adapter uses createSpan + manual end (not withSpan's callback activation) because the structural seam owns lifecycle; the parent linkage is via the remote parent Context passed at startSpan, not ambient activation, so async boundaries do not break parent-child linkage.
Behavior change risk if instrumentation default were non-NOOP. Mitigation: default is explicitly the NOOP SagaInstrumentation; regression test asserts existing runtime tests pass unchanged.
JSR slow-type risk from new exported types. Mitigation: SagaTraceParent and all fields are concrete; no inference; full ./telemetry export-map lint per harness lesson.

Double-instrumentation if both engine and bridge tried to own the span. Mitigation: locked decision — only SagaEngine creates the handle span; the bridge merely holds instrumentation for deferred cascade spans.
OpenHands/Codex worktree edit-redirect landmine (memory): generator must write via the worktree; supervisor reconciles any leaked edits and the lock file before merge.
